content_en:
The Prophet s& used to eat bread with whatever condiment happened to be available. Sometimes he would eat bread with meat, sometimes with watermelon and sometimes with dates. One time, the Prophet b placed a dried date on a piece of bread and said that this, the date, is the condiment of that, the bread. Barley bread is cold and dry while dates are hot and wet, so eating both foods together is one of the wisest decisions, especially for those used to this type of food, such as the people of AIMedina. Sometimes, the Prophet “ would eat bread with vinegar, saying, What a good condiment vinegar is.' This praise for the vinegar came because it was the only available food in the house then, not because vinegar is better than the other types of foods, as some ignorant people think. The Hadith sates that the Prophet as once came to one of his wives and asked if she had any condiment when they offered him some bread. When she said that they only had vinegar, he said, "What a good condiment vinegar is."

Eating bread with a condiment preserves the

——— The Prophetic Medicine

health unlike eating only one type of food. Condiments are called as such because when they are added to the bread, the bread tastes better and the food helps preserve the health.

In addition, the Prophet * used to eat from the fruits that were in season in his land, as this In addition is one of the best methods of preserving health, Allah's wisdom decided that every land has its own fruits that are suitable and beneficial to that land when in season. When the people consume these fruits they will help preserve their health and well being and it serves as several medicines. On the other hand, those who refrain from eating the fruits of their area will be among the most ailing persons and the farthest from good health and well being.

The wetness that the various fruits contain represents the favorable heat of the season and the land. The stomach would then be able to bring the fruits to maturity and would fend off their harm, if one does not eat them excessively causing his nature to bear what it cannot bear. In addition, one should not spoil the food that his stomach is digesting by eating fruits, nor drink water with it. Fruits should be eaten only after the food in the stomach is digested to prevent constipation. Those who consume fruits in the proper manner, in the proper time and in the proper conditions the fruits act as a cure and a

The Prophet's guidance on the proper way to sit while eating

The Prophet b said:

"I do not eat while leaning on my side." He In addition said, “I only sit like the slave sits and eat like the slave eats."

Leaning on the side harms the stomach because it prevents the food

from going down the proper channel and reaching the stomach quickly, as it pressures the stomach and does not allow it to be open properly for the food to pass through.

Eating while leaning on something is the practice of the arrogant people and this is why the Prophet b said that:

"T only eat like the slave eats."

The Prophet b used to it on his feet while eating. He used to place his k iees on tne ground and would place the bottom of his !e* foot or: the upper part of his righi foot in humility for the Lerd and in respect for the food . This is ho pest way to sit for eating,

and for those prese

———. The Prophetic Medicine

because the organs of the body will all be in their proper position, along with observing good manners.

As we have stated, the best way to eat is sitting up with the organs of the body in their natural position. The worst way of eating is leaning on one's side because the windpipe and the chewing organs will be pressured in this position and squeeze the stomach.

In addition, the Prophet b could have meant that he does not sit while leaning on pillows as the arrogant do and those who want to eat more food. Rather, the Prophet as used to eat what is barely sufficient just as the slave does.

The Prophet_b used three fingers while eating

This is one of the best methods of eating. Eating with one or two fingers does not help the appetite or provide sufficient amount of food in each bite, and consequently, one would only be able to finish eating after a long time. In addition, the organs responsible for handling the food would not be comfortable with the fact that they are getting so little food with each bite, in this case the organs will pass through periods of inactivity while one is still eating! If one eats one or two grains at a time, he will neither enjoy eating or feel comfortable. Eating with five fingers sends to the stomach more food than it can handle in each bite. Death might even occur if

one of the organs becomes clogged with excessive food. In addition, the organs of the body will be forced to handle large amounts of food and thus the person would neither enjoy eating it nor would their body be comfortable digesting it. Therefore, the best way of eating is by using three fingers as the Prophet b and those who imitate him do.

The Prophet's guidance on certain combinations of

The Prophet b never mixed fish and milk, milk and sour foods or two hot dishes, or two cold dishes, two sticky dishes, two constipating dishes, two laxative dishes, two heavy dishes, two liquid foods or two types of foods that produced the same condition. In addition, he did not join between two types of food that caused opposite effects, such as constipating and diarthea, or easily digested with heavy foods broiled with cooked foods, dry with fresh foods, eggs and milk or meat and milk. In addition, he neither ate a food when it is rather hot, nor old food that was heated for him the next day. Further, he did not eat any salty or rotten foods, such as pickles. All these types of foods are harmful and cause the health to change for the worst.

The Prophet b In addition used to neutralize the harm of some foods by their opposites, as much as he could. When the food is hot, he neutralizes its effect with

~——— The Prophetic Medicine

a cold food, and when it is dry, he neutralizes it with wet foods, such as eating ripe dates with cucumbers and dried dates with butter. He In addition used to drink the water of soaked dates to neutralize the effect of heavy foods. Further, the Prophet bused to eat dinner even if it only consisted of a few dates.

Abu Nu'aym mention that the Prophet m, used to discourage sleeping just after eating food because it will harden the heart. The doctors In addition advise the people to walk even a few steps after dinner, because sleeping just after dinner is very harmful. Muslim doctors add that one could In addition pray after eating his dinner, so that the food resides in the bottom of the stomach where it will be easily digested.

The Prophet b did not drink [water or milk] with his food, because the drink would spoil the food, especially when the water is hot or cold.

It is not preferred that one drinks water after sports activity, when tired, after sexual intercourse, before and after eating food and atter eating fruit. In addition, it is not preferred that one drinks water after taking a bath and after sleeping. All these activities will harm the health and one should not think about the benefits he might gain from drinking with the food.

The Prophet's guidance on beverages

The Prophet's guidance regarding beverages was In addition the most beneficial for preserving the health. The Prophet b used to drink honey with cold water, and this is an especially effective method to preserve the heath that only the best doctors could have knowledge of. Drinking honey on an empty stomach will dissolve phlegm, purify the stomach's protective layers and dissipate its viscidity and excrements while heating it mildly. Honey In addition helps against the clogs in the stomach, kidney, liver and prostate. Honey is much more profitable to the stomach than any other sweet.

We should state that honey might harm those suffering from bile, because it aggravates it. In this case, the harmful side effect of honey is neutralized when taking it with vinegar, as this method makes the honey very beneficial. In addition, honey is much more beneficial than any other sugar based drink, especially for those who are not used to these types of sweet drinks.

When one drinks sweet drinks that he is not used to, he will not benefit as much as he will benefit from honey, as this is the role that one's customs play. The customs remove foundations and establish foundations..

When the drink is both sweet and cold, it will be most beneficial for the body and one of the best methods

—— The Prophetic Medicine

to preserve its health and the well being of the soul, strength, liver, and heart. In addition, when the drink is both sweet and cold, the body will be eager for it, will benefit from it, nutrition wise, and will quickly digest and transfer it to the other organs of the body.

Cold water is wet, quenches the thirst, preserves the moistures in the body, regenerate the moistures that the body has lost, softens the food and helps transfer it through the veins quickly.

The doctors differed on if water is In addition nutritional. as some of them said that it is nutritious since the body grows and becomes milder and_ stronger, especially when it is in need of water. They In addition said that there are several similarities between man and animals, such as growing and

becoming fairer and stronger. The plants In addition grow and have their own special kind of senses and movements. Since plants feed on water, how can anyone deny that water is nutritious, they asked?

They In addition say that they do not deny that food provides most of the nutritional intake of the body. They only deny that water is not considered of any nutritional value at all. They In addition say that the food only provides nourishment because of the watery parts it contains.

They In addition say that water is the resource of life for animals and plants. There is no doubt, they say, that the substance that is closer to the resource of life will provide more nourishment. How about if the substance is the resource of life itself?
content_id:
Nabi ﷺ biasa makan roti dengan lauk apa pun yang tersedia. Terkadang beliau makan roti dengan daging, terkadang dengan semangka, dan terkadang dengan kurma. Suatu ketika, Nabi ﷺ meletakkan kurma kering di atas sepotong roti dan mengatakan bahwa itulah, kurma, adalah lauk dari roti tersebut. Roti barli bersifat dingin dan kering sementara kurma bersifat panas dan lembab, sehingga memakan kedua makanan tersebut bersama-sama adalah salah satu keputusan terbijak, terutama bagi mereka yang terbiasa dengan jenis makanan ini, seperti penduduk Al-Madinah. Terkadang, Nabi ﷺ makan roti dengan cuka, seraya berkata, "Sebaik-baik lauk adalah cuka." Pujian terhadap cuka ini dikarenakan itulah satu-satunya makanan yang tersedia di rumah saat itu, bukan karena cuka lebih baik dari jenis makanan lainnya, sebagaimana yang disangka oleh sebagian orang yang tidak berpengetahuan. Hadith menyatakan bahwa Nabi ﷺ pernah datang kepada salah seorang istrinya dan bertanya apakah ada lauk ketika mereka menghidangkan roti kepada beliau. Ketika sang istri berkata bahwa mereka hanya punya cuka, beliau berkata, "Sebaik-baik lauk adalah cuka."

Makan roti dengan lauk menjaga

——— Thibbun Nabawi

kesehatan, tidak seperti memakan hanya satu jenis makanan. Lauk disebut demikian karena ketika ditambahkan ke roti, roti menjadi lebih lezat dan makanan tersebut membantu menjaga kesehatan.

Selain itu, Nabi ﷺ biasa memakan buah-buahan yang sedang musim di daerahnya, karena hal ini merupakan salah satu cara terbaik menjaga kesehatan. Hikmah Allah menetapkan bahwa setiap daerah memiliki buah-buahan tersendiri yang sesuai dan bermanfaat bagi daerah itu ketika musimnya tiba. Ketika penduduk mengonsumsi buah-buahan ini, hal itu akan membantu menjaga kesehatan dan kesejahteraan mereka serta berfungsi sebagai berbagai macam obat. Sebaliknya, mereka yang enggan memakan buah-buahan dari daerahnya akan menjadi orang yang paling sering sakit dan paling jauh dari kesehatan serta kesejahteraan yang baik.

Kelembaban yang terkandung dalam berbagai buah-buahan mewakili panas yang sesuai dari musim dan daerah tersebut. Perut kemudian akan mampu mematangkan buah-buahan itu dan menangkal bahayanya, selama seseorang tidak memakannya secara berlebihan hingga melampaui batas kemampuan tubuhnya. Selain itu, seseorang tidak boleh merusak makanan yang sedang dicerna perutnya dengan memakan buah-buahan, dan tidak pula minum air bersamanya. Buah-buahan sebaiknya dimakan hanya setelah makanan di dalam perut telah dicerna, untuk mencegah sembelit. Mereka yang mengonsumsi buah-buahan dengan cara yang tepat, pada waktu yang tepat, dan dalam kondisi yang tepat, buah-buahan itu berfungsi sebagai obat dan

Panduan Nabi tentang cara duduk yang benar saat makan

Nabi ﷺ bersabda:

"Aku tidak makan sambil bersandar." Beliau juga bersabda, "Aku hanya duduk seperti seorang hamba duduk dan makan seperti seorang hamba makan."

Bersandar ke samping membahayakan perut karena menghalangi makanan

turun melalui saluran yang benar dan mencapai perut dengan cepat, karena posisi itu menekan perut dan tidak membiarkannya terbuka dengan baik agar makanan dapat lewat.

Makan sambil bersandar adalah kebiasaan orang-orang yang sombong dan itulah mengapa Nabi ﷺ bersabda:

"Aku hanya makan seperti seorang hamba makan."

Nabi ﷺ biasa duduk di atas kaki beliau saat makan. Beliau menaruh kedua lutut di tanah dan meletakkan bagian bawah kaki kirinya di atas bagian atas kaki kanannya dalam kerendahan hati kepada Allah dan dalam penghormatan terhadap makanan. Inilah cara duduk terbaik untuk makan,

dan bagi mereka yang hadir

———. Thibbun Nabawi

karena seluruh organ tubuh akan berada pada posisi yang tepat, disertai dengan menjaga adab yang baik.

Sebagaimana telah kami sebutkan, cara makan terbaik adalah dengan duduk tegak dengan organ-organ tubuh dalam posisi alaminya. Cara makan terburuk adalah bersandar ke samping karena saluran napas dan organ pengunyah akan tertekan dalam posisi ini dan menekan perut.

Selain itu, Nabi ﷺ mungkin bermaksud bahwa beliau tidak duduk sambil bersandar pada bantal sebagaimana yang dilakukan orang-orang sombong dan mereka yang ingin makan lebih banyak. Sebaliknya, Nabi ﷺ biasa makan sekadar yang mencukupi sebagaimana yang dilakukan seorang hamba.

Nabi ﷺ menggunakan tiga jari saat makan

Inilah salah satu cara makan terbaik. Makan dengan satu atau dua jari tidak membantu nafsu makan atau memberikan jumlah makanan yang cukup dalam setiap suapan, dan akibatnya, seseorang hanya akan mampu menyelesaikan makannya setelah waktu yang lama. Selain itu, organ-organ yang bertanggung jawab menangani makanan tidak akan nyaman dengan kenyataan bahwa mereka mendapatkan begitu sedikit makanan di setiap suapan; dalam hal ini organ-organ tersebut akan melewati periode tidak aktif sementara seseorang masih makan. Jika seseorang makan satu atau dua butir setiap kali, ia tidak akan menikmati makan maupun merasa nyaman. Makan dengan lima jari mengirimkan ke perut lebih banyak makanan dari yang bisa ditanganinya dalam setiap suapan. Kematian bahkan bisa terjadi jika

salah satu organ tersumbat oleh makanan yang berlebihan. Selain itu, organ-organ tubuh akan terpaksa menangani makanan dalam jumlah besar sehingga orang tersebut tidak akan menikmati makannya maupun tubuhnya nyaman mencernanya. Oleh karena itu, cara makan terbaik adalah dengan menggunakan tiga jari sebagaimana yang dilakukan Nabi ﷺ dan mereka yang meneladani beliau.

Panduan Nabi tentang beberapa kombinasi makanan tertentu

Nabi ﷺ tidak pernah mencampur ikan dan susu, susu dan makanan asam, dua hidangan panas, dua hidangan dingin, dua hidangan lengket, dua hidangan yang menyebabkan sembelit, dua hidangan pencahar, dua hidangan berat, dua makanan cair, atau dua jenis makanan yang menghasilkan kondisi yang sama. Selain itu, beliau tidak menggabungkan dua jenis makanan yang memberikan efek berlawanan, seperti makanan yang menyebabkan sembelit dengan yang menyebabkan diare, atau makanan yang mudah dicerna dengan makanan berat, makanan yang dipanggang dengan yang dimasak, makanan kering dengan yang segar, telur dan susu, atau daging dan susu. Beliau juga tidak memakan makanan yang masih terlalu panas, maupun makanan lama yang dipanaskan kembali keesokan harinya. Lebih lanjut, beliau tidak memakan makanan yang sangat asin atau yang sudah busuk, seperti acar. Semua jenis makanan ini berbahaya dan menyebabkan kesehatan berubah menjadi lebih buruk.

Nabi ﷺ juga biasa menetralisir bahaya sebagian makanan dengan lawannya, sebisa mungkin. Ketika makanan bersifat panas, beliau menetralisir efeknya dengan

~——— Thibbun Nabawi

makanan dingin, dan ketika makanan bersifat kering, beliau menetralisirnya dengan makanan basah, seperti memakan kurma matang dengan mentimun dan kurma kering dengan mentega. Beliau juga biasa meminum air rendaman kurma untuk menetralisir efek makanan berat. Lebih lanjut, Nabi ﷺ biasa makan malam meskipun hanya terdiri dari beberapa butir kurma.

Abu Nu'aim menyebutkan bahwa Nabi ﷺ biasa melarang tidur segera setelah makan karena hal itu akan mengeraskan hati. Para dokter juga menganjurkan agar orang berjalan meskipun hanya beberapa langkah setelah makan malam, karena tidur segera setelah makan malam sangat berbahaya. Dokter-dokter Muslim menambahkan bahwa seseorang juga dapat salah setelah makan malamnya, agar makanan menetap di bagian bawah perut di mana ia akan lebih mudah dicerna.

Nabi ﷺ tidak minum [air atau susu] bersama makanannya, karena minuman akan merusak makanan, terutama ketika air tersebut panas atau dingin.

Tidak dianjurkan minum air setelah berolahraga, ketika kelelahan, setelah berhubungan suami istri, sebelum dan sesudah makan, serta setelah makan buah. Selain itu, tidak dianjurkan pula minum air setelah mandi dan setelah tidur. Semua aktivitas ini akan membahayakan kesehatan dan seseorang tidak boleh memikirkan manfaat yang mungkin diperolehnya dari minum bersama makanan.

Panduan Nabi tentang minuman

Panduan Nabi ﷺ mengenai minuman juga merupakan yang paling bermanfaat dalam menjaga kesehatan. Nabi ﷺ biasa meminum madu dengan air dingin, dan ini adalah metode yang sangat efektif untuk menjaga kesehatan yang hanya dapat diketahui oleh dokter-dokter terbaik. Meminum madu dalam keadaan perut kosong akan melarutkan dahak, membersihkan lapisan pelindung perut dan menghilangkan kekentalan serta kotorannya, sambil memanaskannya secara ringan. Madu juga membantu mengatasi penyumbatan pada perut, ginjal, hati, dan prostat. Madu jauh lebih bermanfaat bagi perut daripada pemanis lainnya.

Perlu kami sampaikan bahwa madu dapat membahayakan mereka yang menderita gangguan empedu, karena dapat memperparahnya. Dalam hal ini, efek samping berbahaya dari madu dinetralisir ketika dikonsumsi bersama cuka, karena metode ini menjadikan madu sangat bermanfaat. Selain itu, madu jauh lebih bermanfaat daripada minuman berbasis gula lainnya, terutama bagi mereka yang tidak terbiasa dengan jenis minuman manis tersebut.

Ketika seseorang meminum minuman manis yang tidak biasa ia konsumsi, ia tidak akan memperoleh manfaat sebesar manfaat yang ia peroleh dari madu, karena inilah peran yang dimainkan oleh kebiasaan seseorang. Kebiasaan menghapus landasan dan membangun landasan.

Ketika minuman tersebut sekaligus manis dan dingin, hal itu akan memberikan manfaat paling besar bagi tubuh dan merupakan salah satu cara terbaik

—— Pengobatan Nabawi

untuk menjaga kesehatan serta kesejahteraan jiwa, kekuatan, hati, dan jantung. Selain itu, ketika minuman tersebut sekaligus manis dan dingin, tubuh akan bersemangat mengonsumsinya, akan memperoleh manfaat darinya dari segi gizi, serta akan cepat mencerna dan menyalurkannya ke organ-organ tubuh lainnya.

Air dingin bersifat lembap, menghilangkan dahaga, menjaga kelembapan dalam tubuh, memulihkan kelembapan yang telah hilang dari tubuh, melembutkan makanan, dan membantu menyalurkannya melalui pembuluh darah dengan cepat.

Para dokter berbeda pendapat mengenai apakah air memiliki nilai gizi. Sebagian dari mereka berpendapat bahwa air memang bergizi, karena tubuh tumbuh, menjadi lebih lembut, dan lebih kuat, terutama ketika tubuh membutuhkan air. Mereka juga menyebutkan bahwa terdapat beberapa kesamaan antara manusia dan hewan, seperti pertumbuhan dan

menjadi lebih sehat dan kuat. Demikian pula tumbuhan yang tumbuh dan memiliki jenis indera serta gerakannya sendiri. Karena tumbuhan hidup dari air, bagaimana bisa seseorang menyangkal bahwa air mengandung gizi, demikian argumen mereka?

Mereka juga mengatakan bahwa mereka tidak menyangkal bahwa makanan menyediakan sebagian besar asupan gizi tubuh. Mereka hanya menyangkal bahwa air sama sekali tidak memiliki nilai gizi. Mereka juga mengatakan bahwa makanan hanya memberikan gizi karena kandungan bagian berair yang ada di dalamnya.

Mereka juga mengatakan bahwa air adalah sumber kehidupan bagi hewan dan tumbuhan. Tidak diragukan lagi, kata mereka, bahwa zat yang lebih dekat dengan sumber kehidupan akan memberikan lebih banyak gizi. Apalagi jika zat tersebut adalah sumber kehidupan itu sendiri?

One time, النبي ﷺ b placed a dried date on a piece of bread and said that this, the date, is the condiment of that, the bread. Barley bread is cold and dry while dates are hot and wet, so eating both foods together is one of the wisest decisions, especially for those used to this type of food, such as the people of AIMedina. Sometimes, النبي ﷺ “ would eat bread with vinegar, saying, What a good condiment vinegar is.' This praise for the vinegar came because it was the only available food in the house then, not because vinegar is better than the other types of foods, as some ignorant people think. The الحديث sates that النبي ﷺ as once came to one of his wives and asked if she had any condiment when they offered him some bread. When she said that they only had vinegar, he said, "What a good condiment vinegar is."

health unlike eating only one type of food. Condiments are called as such because when they are added to the bread, the bread tastes better and the food helps preserve the health.

When the people consume these fruits they will help preserve their health and well being and it serves as several medicines. On the other hand, those who refrain from eating the fruits of their area will be among the most ailing persons and the farthest from good health and well being.

النبي ﷺ's guidance on the proper way to sit while eating

النبي ﷺ b said:

"I do not eat while leaning on my side." He In addition said, “I only sit like the slave sits and eat like the slave eats."

Leaning on the side harms the stomach because it prevents the food

from going down the proper channel and reaching the stomach quickly, as it pressures the stomach and does not allow it to be open properly for the food to pass through.

Eating while leaning on something is the practice of the arrogant people and this is why النبي ﷺ b said that:

"T only eat like the slave eats."

and for those prese

———. The Prophetic Medicine

because the organs of the body will all be in their proper position, along with observing good manners.

As we have stated, the best way to eat is sitting up with the organs of the body in their natural position. The worst way of eating is leaning on one's side because the windpipe and the chewing organs will be pressured in this position and squeeze the stomach.

The Prophet_b used three fingers while eating

This is one of the best methods of eating. Eating with one or two fingers does not help the appetite or provide sufficient amount of food in each bite, and consequently, one would only be able to finish eating after a long time. In addition, the organs responsible for handling the food would not be comfortable with the fact that they are getting so little food with each bite, in this case the organs will pass through periods of inactivity while one is still eating! If one eats one or two grains at a time, he will neither enjoy eating or feel comfortable. Eating with five fingers sends to the stomach more food than it can handle in each bite. Death might even occur if

one of the organs becomes clogged with excessive food. In addition, the organs of the body will be forced to handle large amounts of food and thus the person would neither enjoy eating it nor would their body be comfortable digesting it. Therefore, the best way of eating is by using three fingers as النبي ﷺ b and those who imitate him do.

النبي ﷺ b never mixed fish and milk, milk and sour foods or two hot dishes, or two cold dishes, two sticky dishes, two constipating dishes, two laxative dishes, two heavy dishes, two liquid foods or two types of foods that produced the same condition. In addition, he did not join between two types of food that caused opposite effects, such as constipating and diarthea, or easily digested with heavy foods broiled with cooked foods, dry with fresh foods, eggs and milk or meat and milk. In addition, he neither ate a food when it is rather hot, nor old food that was heated for him the next day. Further, he did not eat any salty or rotten foods, such as pickles. ~——— The Prophetic Medicine

a cold food, and when it is dry, he neutralizes it with wet foods, such as eating ripe dates with cucumbers and dried dates with butter. He In addition used to drink the water of soaked dates to neutralize the effect of heavy foods. Further, النبي ﷺ bused to eat dinner even if it only consisted of a few dates.

Abu Nu'aym mention that النبي ﷺ m, used to discourage sleeping just after eating food because it will harden the heart. The doctors In addition advise the people to walk even a few steps after dinner, because sleeping just after dinner is very harmful. Muslim doctors add that one could In addition pray after eating his dinner, so that the food resides in the bottom of the stomach where it will be easily digested.

النبي ﷺ b did not drink [water or milk] with his food, because the drink would spoil the food, especially when the water is hot or cold.

It is not preferred that one drinks water after sports activity, when tired, after sexual intercourse, before and after eating food and atter eating fruit. In addition, it is not preferred that one drinks water after taking a bath and after sleeping. All these activities will harm the health and one should not think about the benefits he might gain from drinking with the food.

النبي ﷺ's guidance on beverages

النبي ﷺ's guidance regarding beverages was In addition the most beneficial for preserving the health. النبي ﷺ b used to drink honey with cold water, and this is an especially effective method to preserve the heath that only the best doctors could have knowledge of. Drinking honey on an empty stomach will dissolve phlegm, purify the stomach's protective layers and dissipate its viscidity and excrements while heating it mildly. Honey In addition helps against the clogs in the stomach, kidney, liver and prostate. Honey is much more profitable to the stomach than any other sweet.

We should state that honey might harm those suffering from bile, because it aggravates it. In this case, the harmful side effect of honey is neutralized when taking it with vinegar, as this method makes the honey very beneficial. In addition, honey is much more beneficial than any other sugar based drink, especially for those who are not used to these types of sweet drinks.

When one drinks sweet drinks that he is not used to, he will not benefit as much as he will benefit from honey, as this is the role that one's customs play. The customs remove foundations and establish foundations..

When the drink is both sweet and cold, it will be most beneficial for the body and one of the best methods

—— The Prophetic Medicine

to preserve its health and the well being of the soul, strength, liver, and heart. In addition, when the drink is both sweet and cold, the body will be eager for it, will benefit from it, nutrition wise, and will quickly digest and transfer it to the other organs of the body.

Cold water is wet, quenches the thirst, preserves the moistures in the body, regenerate the moistures that the body has lost, softens the food and helps transfer it through the veins quickly.

The doctors differed on if water is In addition nutritional. as some of them said that it is nutritious since the body grows and becomes milder and_ stronger, especially when it is in need of water. They In addition said that there are several similarities between man and animals, such as growing and

becoming fairer and stronger. The plants In addition grow and have their own special kind of senses and movements. Since plants feed on water, how can anyone deny that water is nutritious, they asked?

They In addition say that they do not deny that food provides most of the nutritional intake of the body. They only deny that water is not considered of any nutritional value at all. They In addition say that the food only provides nourishment because of the watery parts it contains.

They In addition say that water is the resource of life for animals and plants. There is no doubt, they say, that the substance that is closer to the resource of life will provide more nourishment. How about if the substance is the resource of life itself?
